__data__.text:
Storage  Account  Redundancy  
Specifies  how  data  is  replicated  across  Azure  regions.  
LRS,  GRS,  RAGRS,  ZRS,  GZRS,  RAGZRS  
LRS:  
Locally  Redundant  Storage  (3  copies  within  one  datacenter)  
 GRS:  Geo-Redundant  Storage  (replication  to  a  secondary  region)  
 RAGRS:  Read-Access  GRS  (GRS  +  read  access  to  the  secondary  region)  
ZRS:  Zone-Redundant  Storage  (replication  across  availability  zones)  
GZRS :   
Geo-Zone  Redundant  Storage  (ZRS  +  cross-region)  
RAGZRS:  Read-Access  GZRS  (GZRS  +  read  access  to  secondary)  
  
Premium  supports  only  LRS  &  ZRS;  Standard  supports  all.

__data__.text:
Operation  Description  Command  Example  Command  
Upload  a  Blob  Uploads  a  file  from  local  machine  to  a  container  in  Azure  Blob  Storage.  
az  storage  blob  upload  --account-name  <name>  --account-key  <key>  --container-name  <container>  --name  <blob_name>  --file  <file_path>  
az  storage  blob  upload  --account-name dashstg1zsv  --account-key z0Q6Yw9i7eF8fYABJqzRE+abcd12345abcdefg==  --container-name   logs  --name red1  --file ./myfolder/myfile.txt 
List  Blobs  Lists  all  blobs  stored  in  a  specific  container.  
az  storage  blob  list  --account-name  <name>  --account-key  <key>  --container-name  <container>  --output  table  
az  storage  blob  list  --account-name dashstg1zsv  --account-key z0Q6Yw9i7eF8fYABJqzRE+abcd12345abcdefg== --container-name logs  --output  table  
List  Blobs  with  URLs  Lists  blobs  in  a  container  along  with  accessible  URLs.  
az  storage  blob  list  --account-name  <storage_account_name> --account-key  <  storage_account_primary_access_key  > --container-name  <container_name> --query  '[].  {name:name,  url:join(``,  [`https://<storage_account_name>.blob.core.windows.net/<container_name>/`,  name])}'  --output  table 
az  storage  blob  list  --account-name dashstg1zsv --account-key  z0Q6Yw9i7eF8fYABJqzRE+abcd12345abcdefg== --container-name  logs  --query  '[].{name:name,  url:join(``,  [`https://dashstg1zsv.blob.core.windows.net/logs/`,  name])}'  --output  table

Access
 
the
 
AKS

1.  Copy  the  kubeconfig  output  into  a  file 
      Copy  the  value  shown  under  kube_config output.

    $env:KUBECONFIG  =  "$PWD\kubeconfig-aks.yaml"  
  kubectl  get  nodes

     set  KUBECONFIG=%cd%\kubeconfig-aks.yaml
